---
title: "clinical_var"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "12/2/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)
library(ppcor)

library(reshape2)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
```


```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species1 = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/helios500_clinical.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms", "Papaya", "Orange", "Chapati"), as.numeric))
food_data
nutrition_data
species1
metadata
```

```{r}
colnames(species1)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[2] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species1) * 0.5
species_filtered1 <- species1 %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)
species_filtered1
# species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)
# 
# #abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
# #species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
# #species_filtered2
# species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
# #species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
# #species_filtered2
# food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
# #food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
# nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```


```{r}
unique_species <- read.csv("figure/unique_significant_species.csv")
unique_species$species
colnames(species1)[1] = "Participant_ID"
sig_species <- species1 %>%
  dplyr::select("Participant_ID", all_of(unique_species$species))

colnames(metadata)[2] = "Participant_ID"
```


```{r}
sig_species
metadata
sig_species <- sig_species %>%
  mutate(Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID))

metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID))
```

```{r}
merged_data <- sig_species %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")
merged_data_all = merged_data %>%
  inner_join(food_data, by = "Participant_ID")
merged_data_all$`Bifidobacterium catenulatum`
```
# four groups based on bifido and thosai
```{r}
# Create binary categories using median splits
merged_data_all$bifido_cat <- ifelse(
  merged_data_all$`Bifidobacterium catenulatum` > 
    median(merged_data_all$`Bifidobacterium catenulatum`), 
  "High", "Low"
)

merged_data_all$thosai_cat <- ifelse(
  merged_data_all$Idli_Thosai > median(merged_data_all$Idli_Thosai), 
  "High", "Low"
)

# Create combined groups
merged_data_all$combined_group <- paste(
  merged_data_all$bifido_cat, 
  merged_data_all$thosai_cat
)

# Create a contingency table
contingency_table <- table(
  merged_data_all$combined_group, 
  merged_data_all$diabetes_status
)

# View the contingency table
print(contingency_table)
```


```{r}
# Perform chi-square test
chi_test <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
print(chi_test)
```


```{r}
# Create a visual representation using ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# Convert to percentages
prop_table <- prop.table(contingency_table, margin = 1) * 100

# Convert to data frame for plotting
plot_data <- as.data.frame(prop_table)
colnames(plot_data) <- c("Group", "Diabetes_Status", "Percentage")

# Create stacked bar plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Group, y = Percentage, fill = Diabetes_Status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Diabetes Status by Bifido-Thosai Groups",
    x = "Bifido-Thosai Group",
    y = "Percentage (%)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
# Get summary statistics for each group
library(dplyr)

group_summary <- merged_data_all %>%
  group_by(combined_group, diabetes_status) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    percent = n() / nrow(merged_data_all) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(combined_group, diabetes_status)

print(group_summary)
```
```{r}
# First, create a specific group for high-high vs others
merged_data_all$special_group <- ifelse(
  merged_data_all$bifido_cat == "High" & merged_data_all$thosai_cat == "High",
  "High Bifido & High Thosai",
  "Other Groups"
)

# Create contingency table for this specific comparison
high_high_table <- table(
  merged_data_all$special_group,
  merged_data_all$diabetes_status
)

# Chi-square test
high_high_chi <- chisq.test(high_high_table)
print(high_high_chi)

# Calculate proportions within each group
prop_table <- prop.table(high_high_table, margin = 1) * 100

# Print percentages
print("Percentage of diabetes status in each group:")
print(prop_table)

# Visualize using ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# Convert to data frame for plotting
plot_data <- as.data.frame(prop_table)
colnames(plot_data) <- c("Group", "Diabetes_Status", "Percentage")

# Create bar plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Group, y = Percentage, fill = Diabetes_Status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Diabetes Status Distribution",
    subtitle = "High Bifido & High Thosai vs Other Groups",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Percentage (%)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# You might also want to perform a logistic regression
# This will give you odds ratios
library(stats)

# Create binary outcome for diabetes (1 = diabetes, 0 = normal/pre-diabetes)
merged_data_all$diabetes_binary <- ifelse(
  merged_data_all$diabetes_status == "diabetes", 1, 0
)

# Fit logistic regression
logistic_model <- glm(
  diabetes_binary ~ special_group,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  data = merged_data_all
)

# Print odds ratios
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(logistic_model))
print("Odds Ratios:")
print(odds_ratios)

# Calculate risk ratios
risk_ratio <- function(table) {
  # Risk in high-high group
  risk_high <- table["High Bifido & High Thosai", "diabetes"] / 
    sum(table["High Bifido & High Thosai", ])
  
  # Risk in other groups
  risk_other <- table["Other Groups", "diabetes"] / 
    sum(table["Other Groups", ])
  
  return(risk_high / risk_other)
}

rr <- risk_ratio(high_high_table)
print("Risk Ratio (High-High vs Others):")
print(rr)
```
# only in indian
```{r}
only_i = subset(merged_data_all, FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I")
only_i$`Bifidobacterium catenulatum`
```
```{r}
# Create binary categories using median splits
only_i$bifido_cat <- ifelse(
  only_i$`Bifidobacterium catenulatum` > 
    median(only_i$`Bifidobacterium catenulatum`), 
  "High", "Low"
)

only_i$thosai_cat <- ifelse(
  only_i$Idli_Thosai > median(only_i$Idli_Thosai), 
  "High", "Low"
)

# Create combined groups
only_i$combined_group <- paste(
  only_i$bifido_cat, 
  only_i$thosai_cat
)

# Create a contingency table
contingency_table_i <- table(
  only_i$combined_group, 
  only_i$diabetes_status
)

# View the contingency table
print(contingency_table_i)
```


```{r}
# Perform chi-square test
chi_test <- chisq.test(contingency_table_i)
print(chi_test)
```


```{r}
# Create a visual representation using ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# Convert to percentages
prop_table <- prop.table(contingency_table_i, margin = 1) * 100

# Convert to data frame for plotting
plot_data <- as.data.frame(prop_table)
colnames(plot_data) <- c("Group", "Diabetes_Status", "Percentage")

# Create stacked bar plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Group, y = Percentage, fill = Diabetes_Status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Diabetes Status by Bifido-Thosai Groups",
    x = "Bifido-Thosai Group",
    y = "Percentage (%)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}

# First, create a specific group for high-high vs others
only_i$special_group <- ifelse(
  only_i$bifido_cat == "High" & only_i$thosai_cat == "High",
  "High Bifido & High Thosai",
  "Other Groups"
)

# Create contingency table for this specific comparison
high_high_table <- table(
  only_i$special_group,
  only_i$diabetes_status
)

# Chi-square test
high_high_chi <- chisq.test(high_high_table)
print(high_high_chi)

# Calculate proportions within each group
prop_table <- prop.table(high_high_table, margin = 1) * 100

# Print percentages
print("Percentage of diabetes status in each group:")
print(prop_table)

# Visualize using ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# Convert to data frame for plotting
plot_data <- as.data.frame(prop_table)
colnames(plot_data) <- c("Group", "Diabetes_Status", "Percentage")

# Create bar plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Group, y = Percentage, fill = Diabetes_Status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Diabetes Status Distribution",
    subtitle = "High Bifido & High Thosai vs Other Groups",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Percentage (%)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# You might also want to perform a logistic regression
# This will give you odds ratios
library(stats)

# Create binary outcome for diabetes (1 = diabetes, 0 = normal/pre-diabetes)
only_i$diabetes_binary <- ifelse(
  only_i$diabetes_status == "diabetes", 1, 0
)

# Fit logistic regression
logistic_model <- glm(
  diabetes_binary ~ special_group,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  data = only_i
)

# Print odds ratios
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(logistic_model))
print("Odds Ratios:")
print(odds_ratios)

# Calculate risk ratios
risk_ratio <- function(table) {
  # Risk in high-high group
  risk_high <- table["High Bifido & High Thosai", "diabetes"] / 
    sum(table["High Bifido & High Thosai", ])
  
  # Risk in other groups
  risk_other <- table["Other Groups", "diabetes"] / 
    sum(table["Other Groups", ])
  
  return(risk_high / risk_other)
}

rr <- risk_ratio(high_high_table)
print("Risk Ratio (High-High vs Others):")
print(rr)
```






# heatmaps
```{r}
# Define columns
species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
clinical_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", 
                  "DLAB81_Insulin", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "BMI", "HOMA")

# Initialize matrices for correlations and p-values
cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(clinical_vars))
p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(clinical_vars))

# Calculate partial correlations
for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(clinical_vars)) {
    # Create temporary dataset with ranked variables
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
      clinical = rank(merged_data[[clinical_vars[j]]]),
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    # Calculate partial correlation
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$clinical,
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    # Cap correlation values at ±0.2
    cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
    p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
}

# Add row and column names
rownames(cor_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cor_matrix) <- clinical_vars
rownames(p_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(p_matrix) <- clinical_vars

# Perform clustering
species_dist <- dist(cor_matrix)
species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]

clinical_dist <- dist(t(cor_matrix))
clinical_clust <- hclust(clinical_dist)
clinical_order <- clinical_clust$labels[clinical_clust$order]

# Convert to long format
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
melted_p <- melt(p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Clinical", "Correlation")
melted_cor$Significant <- melted_p$value < 0.05

# Set factor levels based on clustering
melted_cor$Species <- factor(melted_cor$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_cor$Clinical <- factor(melted_cor$Clinical, levels = clinical_order)

# Create the plot
ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Clinical, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spearman Correlations between Species and Clinical Variables",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Clinical Variables",
    y = "Species"
  )

# Save the plot if needed
#ggsave("figure/clinical_species_correlations.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
```

```{r}
library(gridExtra)
merged_data <- sig_species %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# Define columns
species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
continuous_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", 
                    "DLAB81_Insulin", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "BMI", "HOMA")

# Initialize matrices for continuous variables
cont_cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
cont_p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))

# Initialize vectors for diabetes status
diab_effect <- numeric(length(species_cols))
diab_p <- numeric(length(species_cols))

# Calculate correlations for continuous variables
for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(continuous_vars)) {
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
      clinical = rank(merged_data[[continuous_vars[j]]]),
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$clinical,
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    cont_cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
    cont_p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
  
  # Calculate diabetes association
  temp_data <- data.frame(
    species_abundance = merged_data[[species_cols[i]]],
    gender = merged_data$FREG7_Gender,
    age = merged_data$FREG8_Age,
    ethnicity = merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
    diabetes = merged_data$diabetes_status
  )
  
  model <- lm(species_abundance ~ gender + age + ethnicity, data = temp_data)
  species_residuals <- residuals(model)
  
  kw_test <- kruskal.test(species_residuals ~ temp_data$diabetes)
  
  groups <- split(species_residuals, temp_data$diabetes)
  means <- sapply(groups, mean)
  overall_mean <- mean(species_residuals)
  ss_between <- sum(sapply(groups, length) * (means - overall_mean)^2)
  ss_total <- sum((species_residuals - overall_mean)^2)
  eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
  
  if("diabetes" %in% names(means) && "normal" %in% names(means)) {
    direction <- sign(means["diabetes"] - means["normal"])
  } else {
    direction <- 1
  }
  
  diab_effect[i] <- direction * eta_squared
  diab_p[i] <- kw_test$p.value
}

# Prepare continuous variables plot
rownames(cont_cor_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cont_cor_matrix) <- continuous_vars
rownames(cont_p_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cont_p_matrix) <- continuous_vars

# Clustering for continuous variables
species_dist <- dist(cont_cor_matrix)
species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]

clinical_dist <- dist(t(cont_cor_matrix))
clinical_clust <- hclust(clinical_dist)
clinical_order <- clinical_clust$labels[clinical_clust$order]

# Prepare data for plots
# Continuous variables
melted_cont <- melt(cont_cor_matrix)
melted_cont_p <- melt(cont_p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cont) <- c("Species", "Clinical", "Correlation")
melted_cont$Significant <- melted_cont_p$value < 0.05
melted_cont$Species <- factor(melted_cont$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_cont$Clinical <- factor(melted_cont$Clinical, levels = clinical_order)

# Diabetes status
diab_df <- data.frame(
  Species = species_cols,
  Effect = diab_effect,
  P_value = diab_p
)
diab_df$Species <- factor(diab_df$Species, levels = species_order)
diab_df$Significant <- diab_df$P_value < 0.05

# Create continuous variables plot
p1 <- ggplot(melted_cont, aes(x = Clinical, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Species Correlations with Clinical Variables",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Clinical Variables",
    y = "Species",
    fill = "Correlation"
  )

# Create diabetes status plot
p2 <- ggplot(diab_df, aes(y = Species, x = Effect)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = abs(Effect))) +
  #scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(brewer.pal(9, "PuBu"))) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", high = "plum4") +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", ""), x = 0), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Species Associations with Diabetes Status",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Effect Size (Eta Squared)",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Absolute\nEffect Size"
  )

# Arrange plots side by side
grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.5, 1))

# Optionally save the combined plot
ggsave("figure/clinical_diabetes_associations2.pdf", arrangeGrob(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.5, 1)), width = 15, height = 10)
```
## separate ethnicity
```{r}
library(gridExtra)

# Function to perform analysis for one ethnic group
analyze_ethnic_group <- function(merged_data, ethnic_group) {
  # Filter data for the specific ethnic group
  ethnic_data <- merged_data %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic_group)
  
  # Get columns
  species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
  continuous_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", 
                      "DLAB81_Insulin", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "BMI", "HOMA")
  
  # Initialize matrices
  cont_cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
  cont_p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
  diab_effect <- numeric(length(species_cols))
  diab_p <- numeric(length(species_cols))
  
  # Calculate correlations for continuous variables
  for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
    for(j in 1:length(continuous_vars)) {
      temp_data <- data.frame(
        species = rank(ethnic_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
        clinical = rank(ethnic_data[[continuous_vars[j]]]),
        gender = as.numeric(ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
        age = rank(ethnic_data$FREG8_Age)
      )
      
      # Now adjusting only for gender and age
      pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$clinical,
                              temp_data[,c("gender", "age")])
      
      cont_cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
      cont_p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
    }
    
    # Calculate diabetes association
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species_abundance = ethnic_data[[species_cols[i]]],
      gender = ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender,
      age = ethnic_data$FREG8_Age,
      diabetes = ethnic_data$diabetes_status
    )
    
    # Adjust for gender and age only
    model <- lm(species_abundance ~ gender + age, data = temp_data)
    species_residuals <- residuals(model)
    
    kw_test <- kruskal.test(species_residuals ~ temp_data$diabetes)
    
    groups <- split(species_residuals, temp_data$diabetes)
    means <- sapply(groups, mean)
    overall_mean <- mean(species_residuals)
    ss_between <- sum(sapply(groups, length) * (means - overall_mean)^2)
    ss_total <- sum((species_residuals - overall_mean)^2)
    eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
    
    if("diabetes" %in% names(means) && "normal" %in% names(means)) {
      direction <- sign(means["diabetes"] - means["normal"])
    } else {
      direction <- 1
    }
    
    diab_effect[i] <- direction * eta_squared
    diab_p[i] <- kw_test$p.value
  }
  
  # Prepare matrices
  rownames(cont_cor_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(cont_cor_matrix) <- continuous_vars
  rownames(cont_p_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(cont_p_matrix) <- continuous_vars
  
  # Return results
  list(
    cont_cor = cont_cor_matrix,
    cont_p = cont_p_matrix,
    diab_effect = diab_effect,
    diab_p = diab_p
  )
}

# Analyze each ethnic group
results_C <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data, "C")
results_I <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data, "I")
results_M <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data, "M")

# Function to create plots for one ethnic group
create_plots <- function(results, species_cols, continuous_vars, group_name) {
  # Prepare continuous variables plot
  melted_cont <- melt(results$cont_cor)
  melted_cont_p <- melt(results$cont_p)
  colnames(melted_cont) <- c("Species", "Clinical", "Correlation")
  melted_cont$Significant <- melted_cont_p$value < 0.05
  
  # Create continuous variables plot
  p1 <- ggplot(melted_cont, aes(x = Clinical, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                        limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Species Correlations with Clinical Variables -", group_name),
      subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender and Age\n* indicates p < 0.05",
      x = "Clinical Variables",
      y = "Species",
      fill = "Correlation"
    )
  
  # Prepare diabetes plot data
  diab_df <- data.frame(
    Species = species_cols,
    Effect = results$diab_effect,
    P_value = results$diab_p
  )
  diab_df$Significant <- diab_df$P_value < 0.05
  
  # Create diabetes plot
  p2 <- ggplot(diab_df, aes(y = Species, x = Effect)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = abs(Effect))) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", high = "plum4") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", ""), x = 0), 
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Species Associations with Diabetes Status -", group_name),
      subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender and Age\n* indicates p < 0.05",
      x = "Effect Size (Eta Squared)",
      y = NULL,
      fill = "Absolute\nEffect Size"
    )
  
  list(continuous = p1, diabetes = p2)
}
```


```{r}
# Create plots for each ethnic group
plots_C <- create_plots(results_C, species_cols, continuous_vars, "Chinese")
plots_I <- create_plots(results_I, species_cols, continuous_vars, "Indian")
plots_M <- create_plots(results_M, species_cols, continuous_vars, "Malay")

# plots_C
# plots_I 
# plots_M
```


```{r}
# Arrange all plots
# Continuous variables plots on top row, diabetes plots on bottom row
grid.arrange(
  plots_C$continuous, plots_I$continuous, plots_M$continuous,
  plots_C$diabetes, plots_I$diabetes, plots_M$diabetes,
  ncol = 3, nrow = 2
)


ggsave("figure/ethnic_stratified_analysis.pdf",
       arrangeGrob(plots_C$continuous, plots_I$continuous, plots_M$continuous,
                  plots_C$diabetes, plots_I$diabetes, plots_M$diabetes,
                  ncol = 3, nrow = 2),
       width = 24, height = 16)
```

```{r}
# Create summary table
diabetes_dist <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, diabetes_status) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  # Calculate percentages within each ethnic group
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count/sum(count) * 100)

# Create a plot
ggplot(diabetes_dist, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = count, fill = diabetes_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Diabetes Status by Ethnic Group",
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Diabetes Status"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# Print numerical summary
print(diabetes_dist)
```

## overall analysis for clinical variables
```{r}

# Match samples and create merged dataset
merged_data <- species_filtered1 %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# Print summary of merged data
print("Summary of merged dataset:")
print(paste("Number of samples:", nrow(merged_data)))
print("Samples per diabetes status:")
print(table(merged_data$diabetes_status))
print("Samples per ethnic group:")
print(table(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group))

# Get species columns (all columns from species_filtered1 except Participant_ID)
species_cols <- setdiff(colnames(species_filtered1), "Participant_ID")
print("Number of species columns:")
print(length(species_cols))

# Verify species columns exist in merged_data
missing_cols <- species_cols[!species_cols %in% colnames(merged_data)]
if(length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("Missing species columns in merged data: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse=", "))
}

# Extract species abundance matrix
species_matrix <- as.matrix(merged_data[, species_cols])

# Z-score normalize species abundance
species_matrix_z <- scale(species_matrix)
merged_data[, species_cols] <- species_matrix_z

# Convert categorical variables to factors
merged_data$diabetes_status <- factor(merged_data$diabetes_status, 
                                    levels = c("normal", "pre-diabetes", "diabetes"))
merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- factor(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)

# Function to calculate alpha diversity
calculate_alpha_diversity <- function(abundance_matrix) {
  # Ensure non-negative values for diversity calculations
  abundance_matrix[abundance_matrix < 0] <- 0
  
  alpha_div <- data.frame(
    shannon = diversity(abundance_matrix, index = "shannon"),
    simpson = diversity(abundance_matrix, index = "simpson"),
    observed_otus = specnumber(abundance_matrix)
  )
  return(alpha_div)
}
species_matrix
# Calculate alpha diversity
alpha_div <- calculate_alpha_diversity(species_matrix)
alpha_div$diabetes_status <- merged_data$diabetes_status
alpha_div$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group

# Plot alpha diversity
alpha_plot <- alpha_div %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(shannon, simpson, observed_otus),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = diabetes_status, y = value, fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Diabetes Status", 
       y = "Alpha Diversity Value",
       fill = "Ethnic Group") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

# Save alpha diversity plot
ggsave("alpha_diversity.pdf", alpha_plot, width = 12, height = 6)

# Calculate beta diversity (Bray-Curtis dissimilarity)
beta_dist <- vegdist(species_matrix, method = "bray")

# Perform PCoA
pcoa_result <- cmdscale(beta_dist, k = 2, eig = TRUE)
var_explained <- round(100 * pcoa_result$eig / sum(pcoa_result$eig), 2)

# Create PCoA plot
beta_plot <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pcoa_result$points[,1],
  PC2 = pcoa_result$points[,2],
  diabetes_status = merged_data$diabetes_status,
  FREG5_Ethnic_Group = merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = diabetes_status, shape = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = interaction(diabetes_status, FREG5_Ethnic_Group)), 
               level = 0.95, linetype = 2) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "%)"),
       y = paste0("PC2 (", var_explained[2], "%)"),
       color = "Diabetes Status",
       shape = "Ethnic Group")

# Save beta diversity plot
ggsave("beta_diversity.pdf", beta_plot, width = 10, height = 8)

# Perform PERMANOVA
permanova_result <- adonis2(beta_dist ~ diabetes_status * FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
                          data = merged_data, permutations = 999)
print("PERMANOVA results:")
print(permanova_result)

# Create abundance heatmap
# Calculate mean abundance for each combination
abundance_summary <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(diabetes_status, FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(species_cols), mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Prepare matrix for heatmap
plot_matrix <- as.matrix(abundance_summary[, species_cols])
rownames(plot_matrix) <- paste(abundance_summary$diabetes_status, 
                             abundance_summary$FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
                             sep = "_")

# Create annotation data frame
annotation_df <- data.frame(
  diabetes_status = abundance_summary$diabetes_status,
  FREG5_Ethnic_Group = abundance_summary$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
  row.names = rownames(plot_matrix)
)

# Define annotation colors
annotation_colors <- list(
  diabetes_status = c(normal = "#4DAF4A", 
                     `pre-diabetes` = "#FF7F00", 
                     diabetes = "#E41A1C"),
  FREG5_Ethnic_Group = c(C = "#377EB8", 
                        I = "#984EA3", 
                        M = "#FF7F00",
                        O = "#A65628")
)

# Create and save heatmap
pdf("abundance_heatmap.pdf", width = 12, height = 8)
pheatmap(plot_matrix,
         scale = "none",  # already z-scored
         color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "red"))(100),
         annotation_row = annotation_df,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
         main = "Z-scored Species Abundance Heatmap",
         fontsize_row = 8,
         fontsize_col = 6,
         angle_col = 45,
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         show_colnames = FALSE)
dev.off()

# Perform statistical tests
# Kruskal-Wallis test for alpha diversity
kruskal_results <- lapply(c("shannon", "simpson", "observed_otus"), function(metric) {
  kruskal.test(as.formula(paste(metric, "~ diabetes_status")), data = alpha_div)
})
names(kruskal_results) <- c("shannon", "simpson", "observed_otus")
print("Kruskal-Wallis test results:")
print(kruskal_results)

# Stratified analysis by ethnic group
# Only analyze groups with sufficient samples (e.g., >30)
ethnic_groups_to_analyze <- names(table(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group))[table(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group) > 30]

for(ethnic in ethnic_groups_to_analyze) {
  subset_data <- merged_data[merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic, ]
  
  # Calculate alpha diversity for subset
  subset_matrix <- as.matrix(subset_data[, species_cols])
  subset_alpha <- calculate_alpha_diversity(subset_matrix)
  subset_alpha$diabetes_status <- subset_data$diabetes_status
  
  # Kruskal-Wallis test for subset
  subset_kruskal <- lapply(c("shannon", "simpson", "observed_otus"), function(metric) {
    kruskal.test(as.formula(paste(metric, "~ diabetes_status")), data = subset_alpha)
  })
  names(subset_kruskal) <- c("shannon", "simpson", "observed_otus")
  
  print(paste("\nResults for ethnic group:", ethnic))
  print(subset_kruskal)
}

# Save all results to RData file
save(merged_data, alpha_div, permanova_result, kruskal_results, 
     file = "microbiome_analysis_results.RData")
```


```{r}
# Assuming merged_data is already loaded and contains species and metadata columns
# Create OTU Table
OTU <- otu_table(as.matrix(merged_data[, species_cols]), taxa_are_rows = FALSE)

# Make sure the row names of the OTU table are the Participant IDs
rownames(OTU) <- merged_data$Participant_ID

# Create Sample Data
# Ensure that the sample_data dataframe has Participant_ID as row names
samples_data <- sample_data(merged_data[, c("diabetes_status", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "DLAB81_Insulin", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "BMI")])
rownames(samples_data) <- merged_data$Participant_ID

# Create a phyloseq object
physeq <- phyloseq(OTU, samples_data)
```


```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)

alpha_diversity <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Shannon")
# Convert rownames to a column for merging
alpha_diversity$Participant_ID <- rownames(alpha_diversity)

# Extract sample metadata from the phyloseq object and make it a regular data frame
sample_metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq))
# Ensure 'Participant_ID' is a column for merging
sample_metadata$Participant_ID <- rownames(sample_metadata)

# Merge alpha diversity data with sample metadata
alpha_diversity_full <- merge(alpha_diversity, sample_metadata, by = "Participant_ID")


# Now you can plot or analyze your merged data
# For example, to visualize alpha diversity across diabetes status:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(alpha_diversity_full, aes(x = diabetes_status, y = Shannon)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Alpha Diversity across Diabetes Status", y = "Shannon Diversity Index", x = "Diabetes Status")

```
